function.name: _app_get_define_object
function.description:
Accurately get a single object structure metadata (Define Object) in the application definition based on the object id.

【Purpose】
- AI accurately reads a certain object structure
- Structure confirmation before modification
- Difference analysis
- Dependency impact analysis

【Important Notes】
- Only used for reading structure
- Does not modify any data
- Must clearly specify scope and version

【Hierarchy Rules (Must Follow)】

1️⃣ App-level objects:
- scope must be filled with "App"

2️⃣ Module-level objects:
- scope must be filled with the id of the corresponding ModuleDefine
- Cannot be empty

【Version Rules】
- isDraft = true  → Read draft version
- isDraft = false → Read published version

【Mandatory Rules】
- id, scope, isDraft are all required
- Cannot assume scope out of thin air
- If scope and object hierarchy do not match, must report error

scope.description:
Parent scope identifier (Parent Scope Identifier).

- App-level objects: fill in "App"
- Module-level objects: fill in the id of the corresponding ModuleDefine

isDraft.description:
Whether to read the draft version. true=draft version, false=published version. Can be used to compare differences between draft and published versions.

⚠️ Note: This interface only reads the specified version and will not automatically compare the two versions. If comparison is needed, it must be called twice separately.
